replication_study.hypothesis: Higher individual concern about immigration (imm_concern) is associated with greater distrust in the national parliament (trstprl_rev) among European Social Survey Round 5 respondents, net of standard individual-, country-round- and country-level controls.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: ESS Round 5 (2010–2011) – processed files data_clean_5pct.rds & data_imp_5pct.rds
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Round 5; 14 countries; 5 % random subset for complete-case file; multiply-imputed version with m = 5
data_plan.sample_size: 1 279 individuals in data_clean_5pct.rds (complete cases); 1 279 × 5 imputations in data_imp_5pct.rds
data_plan.access_details: ESS data are publicly available via https://ess-search.nsd.no. Pre-processed RDS files supplied in /app/data/original/13/input/replication_data.

qualification.explanation: The dataset is drawn from the same large-scale survey series as the original study but covers a later wave, giving an independent temporal sample while preserving questionnaire design and variable wording.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Both rely on ESS items for trust in parliament and immigration concern, identical coding documented in the replication script (data_analysis_code.R lines 37–41) and variables listed in post_registration.json (“outcome_variable”, “independent_variables”).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original study analysed ESS Rounds 1-4 (2002-09) (post_registration.json, data.source field). Replication uses Round 5 data only (year variable shows 2010–11: dataset summary) and a different country set (14 vs 23 originally), making it a new sample.

data_plan.notes: Variables are already reverse-coded (suffix _rev) and concern index (imm_concern) pre-computed. Weight variable pspwght is available. Some income variables have ~24 % missing, addressed via listwise deletion (complete-case file) or multiple imputation (data_imp_5pct).

planned_method.steps:
1) Load data_clean_5pct.rds.
2) Fit multilevel linear mixed model with random intercepts by country, weighted by pspwght.
3) Extract coefficient, standard error and p-value for imm_concern.
4) Run robustness checks: (a) no weights; (b) multiply-imputed dataset data_imp_5pct.rds pooled with mice::pool().
5) Compare sign, magnitude, and significance with original coefficients.
planned_method.models: Random-intercept linear mixed model (lme4::lmer); pooled mixed models over multiple imputations (mice + lme4).
planned_method.outcome_variable: trstprl_rev (higher values = higher distrust in parliament)
planned_method.independent_variables: imm_concern (index of three reverse-coded immigration impact items)
planned_method.control_variables: happy_rev, stflife_rev, sclmeet_rev, distrust_soc, stfeco_rev, hincfel, stfhlth_rev, stfedu_rev, vote_gov, vote_frparty, lrscale, hhinc_std, agea, educ, female, vote_share_fr, socexp, lt_imm_cntry, wgi, gdppc, unemp
planned_method.tools_software: R (≥4.0) with lme4, mice, tidyverse (optional for data wrangling)

planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test (lmer’s Wald z/t statistic) with α = 0.05 two-tailed

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Primary analysis uses complete-case file (listwise deletion); robustness uses multiple imputation (m=5) and Rubin’s rules.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal predictor; no adjustment needed. Secondary models reported without corrections.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication successful if imm_concern coefficient is positive and statistically significant (p < .05) in the weighted model; magnitude broadly comparable (within ±50 %) to original ~0.17.

files.data_analysis_code.R: Main script that (a) loads processed RDS data, (b) fits four lmer models (weighted/unweighted; complete-case/imputed), (c) prints model summaries with focal coefficient. Embedded comments document each step.

codebase.notes: Script expects working directory where data RDS files are located (we ensure /app/data). Requires R packages lme4 and mice.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Sign, direction and p-value of imm_concern coefficient; descriptive overlap of control estimates; model R² / REML criterion comparison optional.
